segments.md:
# The Declare homepage

The page you are reading, written in Declare. The design tokens live once as
semantic type components, and the page is a vertical **flow** of auto-sizing
sections — no absolute-y. Each section's height falls out of its content, so
the page stacks and re-flows on its own at any width. The full-bleed chrome —
the blueprint grid, the sticky header, the pointer dot — sits absolutely
around the centered column.

`{ }` bodies are TypeScript, so colors there are `0x…`; `[ ]` literal slots
take `#…`. Every colour on the page has a name in the App's `theme` record —
the palette lives there, once, and nowhere else.

segments.html:
font Sans [ family = "system-ui" ]

font Mono [ family = "ui-monospace" ]

segments.md:
## Type as tokens

Every text style on the page is one of these classes — headings semibold with
tracking that scales with the size, eyebrows mono and wide-tracked, lead and
body in the muted tone — so the page's whole typography reskins from here.

segments.html:
class Heading extends Text [ fontWeight = semibold, textColor = { theme.text },
    letterSpacing = { -0.02 * fontSize }, selectable = true,
    ]


class Eyebrow extends Text [ fontFamily = [Mono, "monospace"], fontSize = 13,
    fontWeight = semibold, letterSpacing = 1.6, textColor = { theme.muted }, selectable = true,
    ]


// the accent section number

class Marker extends Eyebrow [ textColor = { theme.accent } ]

// footer text in the Declare typeface (App Sans), not the mono eyebrow

class FootLabel extends Text [ fontSize = 13, fontWeight = semibold, textColor = { theme.muted }, selectable = true ]

class Lead extends Text [ fontSize = 19, fontWeight = normal, textColor = { theme.lead }, selectable = true ]

class Body extends Text [ fontSize = 17, fontWeight = normal, textColor = { theme.muted }, selectable = true ]

// an article paragraph (the "Why" view) — HTML flow, so its `"""` source wraps
// at the writer's margin and the line breaks collapse away in the rendering

class Para extends HTMLText [ fontSize = 18, lineHeight = 1.2, width = { parent.width },
    bodyColor = { theme.lead }, selectable = true,
    ]

segments.md:
## The header pills

A nav link is a padded pill: it fills with surface-2 on hover (a rounded rect
behind the text, not just a colour change), and `pressed` deepens the pill and
lights the label — the tap feedback a touch device gets in place of hover.
Width auto-fits the label plus 12px of padding each side.

segments.html:
class NavLink extends View [ height = 33, cornerRadius = 8,
    label:   string = "",
    url:     string = "",
    to:      string = "",
    hovered: boolean = false,
    pressed: boolean = false,

    // pressed deepens the pill + lights the label — the tap feedback a touch
    // device gets in place of hover (mouseDown/Up fire on a tap, hover does not).

    fill = { pressed ? theme.pillPress : hovered ? theme.pillHover : null },
    onMouseOver() { hovered = true },
    onMouseOut()  { hovered = false; pressed = false },
    onMouseDown() { pressed = true },
    onMouseUp()   { pressed = false },

    // A pill goes one of two ways: `to` is an in-app location (a fragment — write
    // `app.location`); `url` is an out-of-app target (the `navigate` service action,
    // capabilities.md §6). One or the other, never both.

    onClick()     { if (classroot.to != "") app.location = classroot.to; else if (classroot.url != "") app.navigate(classroot.url) },
    lbl: Text [ x = 12, y = 8, fontFamily = [Sans, "system-ui", "sans-serif"], fontSize = 14,
        text = { classroot.label },
        textColor = { classroot.hovered || classroot.pressed ? theme.text : theme.muted } ],
    width = { this.lbl.width + 24 },
    ]

segments.md:
## Section furniture

The repeated shapes the numbered sections are built from: an idea `Card`
(§01), a `Point` with its accent top-rule and a big gradient `Metric` (§02),
and the footer's separator `Dot`.

segments.html:
class Card extends View [ width = { Math.min(357, parent.width) }, height = 172, cornerRadius = 10,
    stroke = { stroke(1, theme.line) },
    title: string = "",
    body:  string = "",
    muted: boolean = false,
    fill = { muted ? theme.surfaceDeep : theme.surface },
    pad: View [ x = 22, y = 22, width = { parent.width - 44 },
        layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = y, spacing = 14 ],
        Heading [ fontSize = 19, text = { classroot.title } ],
        Body    [ fontSize = 15, width = { parent.width }, text = { classroot.body } ],
        ],
    ]


class Point extends View [ width = { Math.min(357, parent.width) },
    ptitle: string = "",
    body:   string = "",
    layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = y, spacing = 16 ],
    rule: View [ width = { parent.width }, height = 2, fill = { theme.pointRule } ],
    txt: View [ width = { parent.width },
        layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = y, spacing = 8 ],
        Heading [ fontSize = 17, text = { classroot.ptitle } ],
        Body    [ fontSize = 15, width = { parent.width }, text = { classroot.body } ],
        ],
    ]


// width/fontSize key off parent.width (the real available width in the row) —
// a component sizes off its parent, not the app; `app.width` is the whole page

class Metric extends View [ width = { parent.width < 560 ? parent.width : (parent.width - 48) / 2 },
    value: string = "",
    note:  string = "",
    layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = y, spacing = 8 ],
    Heading [ fontSize = { parent.width < 420 ? 54 : 92 }, width = { parent.width }, textAlign = center,
        textFill = { theme.brand }, text = { classroot.value } ],
    Body [ fontSize = 14, width = { parent.width }, textAlign = center, text = { classroot.note } ],
    ]


class Dot extends Body [ fontSize = 14, textColor = { theme.faint }, text = "·" ]

segments.md:
## Buttons

Primary fills accent and brightens on hover; secondary lifts to surface-2 and
answers a press with an accent ring. Press feedback is what a touch device
sees in place of hover — both are driven by the view's own handlers.

segments.html:
class Button extends View [ height = 46, cornerRadius = 9,
    label:   string = "",
    primary: boolean = true,
    hovered: boolean = false,
    pressed: boolean = false,

    fill = { primary ? (pressed ? theme.accentPress : hovered ? theme.accentHover : theme.accent)
                     : (pressed ? theme.press       : hovered ? theme.hover       : theme.surface) },
    stroke = { primary ? stroke(0, theme.accent) : stroke(1, pressed ? theme.accent2 : theme.line) },
    onMouseOver() { hovered = true },
    onMouseOut()  { hovered = false; pressed = false },
    onMouseDown() { pressed = true },
    onMouseUp()   { pressed = false },

    // label centered in the button box (textAlign over the full width)

    caption: Text [ y = 14, width = { parent.width }, textAlign = center, fontSize = 15, fontWeight = semibold,
        text = { classroot.label },
        textColor = { classroot.primary ? theme.ink : theme.text } ],
    ]

segments.md:
## The site footer

One line, shared by every page of the site — the scrolling front page and the
in-app Why / Language views alike: provenance left, legal right.

A `FootLink` is the footer's link rendition: the eyebrow treatment with the
teal hover and the brighter pressed tap feedback, navigating on click.

segments.html:
class FootLink extends FootLabel [
    to: string = "",
    hovered: boolean = false,
    pressed: boolean = false,
    textColor = { pressed ? theme.accent2Press : hovered ? theme.accent2 : theme.muted },
    onMouseOver() { hovered = true },
    onMouseOut()  { hovered = false; pressed = false },
    onMouseDown() { pressed = true },
    onMouseUp()   { pressed = false },
    onClick()     { app.navigate(to) },
    ]


class Footer extends View [ width = { parent.width },
    layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = y, spacing = 16 ],
    rule: View [ width = { parent.width }, height = 1, fill = { theme.line } ],
    row: View [ width = { parent.width }, height = { this.left.height },

        // two groups on one line: provenance LEFT, legal RIGHT (x derives
        // from the group's own measured width — right-justified reactively)

        left: View [ layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = x, spacing = 12 ],

            // one text RUN, not two spaced views — HTMLText carries the brand
            // accent inline, so "Built in Declare" keeps a word space, not a gap

            built: HTMLText [ width = 104, fontSize = 13, fontWeight = semibold, bodyColor = { theme.muted },
                accents = { ({ g: theme.brand }) },
                html = "Built in <span class='g'>Declare</span>" ],
            FootLabel [ textColor = { theme.faint }, text = "·" ],

            // source affordance in the same eyebrow treatment

            src: FootLink [ to = "apps/homepage/homepage.declare?viewer", text = "View Source" ],
            ],

        right: View [ x = { parent.width - this.width },
            layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = x, spacing = 12 ],

            // the deep artifacts, then the legal line; the license is a real
            // artifact (repo-root LICENSE) — the label opens it

            FootLink [ to = "apps/docs/docs.declare", text = "Docs" ],
            FootLabel [ textColor = { theme.faint }, text = "·" ],
            FootLink [ to = "https://github.com/davidtemkin/declarelang", text = "GitHub" ],
            FootLabel [ textColor = { theme.faint }, text = "·" ],
            lic: FootLink [ to = "https://github.com/davidtemkin/declarelang/blob/main/LICENSE", text = "MIT License" ],
            FootLabel [ textColor = { theme.faint }, text = "·" ],
            FootLabel [ text = "© 2026 David Temkin" ],
            ],
        ],
    ]

segments.md:
## The live panels

The heart of the page: every demo is real Declare, compiled and running. A
`CodeField` is a native editable field seeded (uncontrolled, `initial`) from
the demo's source — it follows the source until you type, then holds the
edit, publishing each keystroke on `App.liveSource` for the host to
recompile. A `SourcePanel` frames it (LIVE·EDITABLE chip, Revert, hairline);
a `PreviewFrame` hosts the compiled app as an **embedded child app** — no
iframe — so the preview can't drift from the source. Its `active` flag gates
the island slot, which is what bounds the full-page-editor recursion: a
closed editor's preview renders nothing, so the page source containing that
very editor only descends one level per user click.

segments.html:
class CodeField extends TextInput [ multiline = true, spellcheck = false,
    fontFamily = [Mono, "monospace"], fontSize = 11, textColor = { theme.lead },
    editName: string = "",
    src: string = "",
    initial = { src },
    onInput() { app.liveCard = editName; app.liveSource = text },
    ]


// `height` is set per demo (at the use site) to sit just above what the code /
// preview needs; Revert restores the pristine `src` and asks the host to recompile

class SourcePanel extends View [ width = { parent.width < 760 ? parent.width : Math.floor((parent.width - 20) * 0.58) },
    editName: string = "",
    src: string = "",
    fill = { theme.panel }, cornerRadius = 12, stroke = { stroke(1, theme.panelLine) },

    // the affordance chip: this code runs, and you can type in it

    live: View [ x = 12, y = 7, height = 18, cornerRadius = 5, fill = { theme.accent },
        width = { this.t.width + 14 },
        t: Text [ x = 7, y = 4, fontFamily = [Sans, "system-ui"], fontSize = 9, fontWeight = bold,
            letterSpacing = 0.8, wrap = false, textColor = { theme.ink }, text = "LIVE · EDITABLE" ],
        ],
    revert: View [ y = 5, height = 20, cornerRadius = 6, stroke = { stroke(1, theme.revertLine) },
        hovered: boolean = false,
        x = { parent.width - this.width - 10 },
        width = { this.rlbl.width + 16 },
        fill = { hovered ? theme.hover : null },
        onMouseOver() { hovered = true },
        onMouseOut()  { hovered = false },
        onClick() { classroot.field.text = classroot.src
                    app.liveCard = classroot.editName
                    app.liveSource = classroot.src },
        rlbl: Text [ x = 8, y = 3, fontFamily = [Sans, "system-ui"], fontSize = 11, fontWeight = semibold,
            textColor = { parent.hovered ? theme.text : theme.lead }, text = "Revert" ],
        ],
    hairline: View [ y = 31, width = { parent.width }, height = 1, fill = { theme.panelLine } ],
    field: CodeField [ x = 12, y = 40, width = { parent.width - 24 }, height = { parent.height - 48 },
        editName = { classroot.editName }, src = { classroot.src } ],

    // the compile report, at the panel — a broken edit shows its error right here
    // (the last good preview keeps running beside it); host feeds app.liveReport

    err: View [ width = { parent.width }, fill = #2A1518,
        visible = { app.liveCard == classroot.editName && app.liveReport != "" },
        y = { parent.height - this.height },
        height = { this.msg.height + 16 },
        bar: View [ x = 0, y = 0, width = 3, height = { parent.height }, fill = #E0525E ],
        msg: Text [ x = 14, y = 8, width = { parent.width - 28 }, wrap = true,
            fontFamily = [Mono, "monospace"], fontSize = 11, textColor = #F2A0A6,
            text = { app.liveReport } ],
        ],
    ]


class PreviewFrame extends View [ width = { parent.width < 760 ? parent.width : Math.floor((parent.width - 20) * 0.42) },
    runName: string = "",
    active: boolean = true,
    run: DOMIsland [ width = { parent.width }, height = { parent.height },
        slot = { classroot.active ? "run:" + classroot.runName : "" } ],
    ]

segments.md:
## The whole-page editor

The same `CodeField` + embedded preview as the demo cards, at page scale: the
page's own source on the left, the page it compiles to on the right. It's a
full-window overlay shown when `app.editing` — it reads the App's own size
through the `app` noun, so it always fills whatever the page fills.
`pageSource` is threaded in from the host (`demoSources.__page__` — the
page's own text), and the preview island is `active` only while open, so the
recursion stays bounded by clicks.

segments.html:
class FullPageEditor extends View [ width = { app.width }, height = { app.height },
    open: boolean = false,
    pageSource: string = "",

    // Escape closes the editor — a lifetime-managed Keys subscription (the
    // body gates on `open`, since the raw keyboard fires regardless)

    onKeyUp(e) <- Keys { if (open && e.key == "Escape") app.editing = false },

    // the divider x: 0 = not dragged yet (use the 46% default); the grip writes it.

    split: number = 0,
    splitX: number = { split > 0 ? split : Math.floor(app.width * 0.46) },
    visible = { open },
    fill = { theme.ink },
    bar: View [ width = { parent.width }, height = 44, fill = { theme.surface },
        edge: View [ y = 43, width = { parent.width }, height = 1, fill = { theme.line } ],
        name: Text [ x = 22, y = 14, fontFamily = [Mono, "monospace"], fontSize = 13, fontWeight = semibold,
            textColor = { theme.text }, text = "homepage.declare" ],
        hint: Text [ x = 132, y = 15, fontSize = 12, italic = true, textColor = { theme.dim },
            text = "the whole page — edit and it re-renders live" ],
        close: View [ y = 8, height = 28, cornerRadius = 8, stroke = { stroke(1, theme.line) },
            hovered: boolean = false,
            x = { parent.width - this.width - 16 },
            width = { this.lbl.width + 26 },
            fill = { hovered ? theme.hover : theme.surface },
            onMouseOver() { hovered = true },
            onMouseOut()  { hovered = false },
            onClick()     { app.editing = false },
            lbl: Text [ x = 13, y = 6, fontSize = 13, textColor = { theme.text }, text = "Close ✕" ],
            ],

        // Revert — the pristine page source back in the editor, recompiled

        revert: View [ y = 8, height = 28, cornerRadius = 8, stroke = { stroke(1, theme.line) },
            hovered: boolean = false,
            x = { parent.close.x - this.width - 10 },
            width = { this.lbl.width + 26 },
            fill = { hovered ? theme.hover : theme.surface },
            onMouseOver() { hovered = true },
            onMouseOut()  { hovered = false },
            onClick()     { classroot.code.text = classroot.pageSource
                            app.liveCard = "__page__"
                            app.liveSource = classroot.pageSource },
            lbl: Text [ x = 13, y = 6, fontSize = 13, textColor = { theme.text }, text = "Revert" ],
            ],
        ],

    // source: no wrap (long lines scroll horizontally) + 16px inset off the edge

    code: CodeField [ x = 0, y = 44, width = { classroot.splitX }, height = { parent.height - 44 },
        wrap = false, padding = 16,
        editName = "__page__", src = { classroot.pageSource } ],

    // draggable divider — captures the pointer on press, writes `split` as it moves

    grip: View [ x = { classroot.splitX }, y = 44, width = 6, height = { parent.height - 44 },
        dragging: boolean = false,
        hovered: boolean = false,
        fill = { dragging || hovered ? theme.accent2 : theme.line },
        onMouseOver()  { hovered = true },
        onMouseOut()   { hovered = false },
        onMouseDown()  { dragging = true },
        onMouseMove(e) { if (dragging) classroot.split = Math.max(240, Math.min(app.width - 320, e.x)) },
        onMouseUp()    { dragging = false },
        ],
    preview: PreviewFrame [ x = { classroot.splitX + 6 }, y = 44,
        width = { parent.width - classroot.splitX - 6 }, height = { parent.height - 44 },
        runName = "__page__", active = { classroot.open } ],

    // the compile report for a broken page edit, over the bottom of the code pane

    err: View [ x = 0, width = { classroot.splitX }, fill = #2A1518,
        visible = { classroot.open && app.liveCard == "__page__" && app.liveReport != "" },
        y = { parent.height - this.height },
        height = { this.msg.height + 16 },
        bar: View [ x = 0, y = 0, width = 3, height = { parent.height }, fill = #E0525E ],
        msg: Text [ x = 16, y = 8, width = { parent.width - 32 }, wrap = true,
            fontFamily = [Mono, "monospace"], fontSize = 12, textColor = #F2A0A6,
            text = { app.liveReport } ],
        ],
    ]

segments.md:
## Backdrop and section heads

The blueprint grid draws itself; a `SectionHead` is the tight
number · heading · lead rhythm every numbered section opens with.

segments.html:
class GridBg extends View [
    draw(d) {
        d.fillStyle = theme.grid
        for (let gx = 0; gx <= width;  gx += 32) d.fillRect(gx, 0, 1, height)
        for (let gy = 0; gy <= height; gy += 32) d.fillRect(0, gy, width, 1)
        },
    ]


class SectionHead extends View [ width = { parent.width },
    num:  string = "",
    head: string = "",
    lead: string = "",
    layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = y, spacing = 14 ],
    Marker  [ text = { classroot.num } ],
    Heading [ fontSize = 40, width = { Math.min(900, parent.width) }, text = { classroot.head } ],
    Lead    [ width = { Math.min(760, parent.width) }, text = { classroot.lead } ],
    ]

segments.md:
## The page

The palette lives once as a prevailing `theme` token record — every semantic
type class defaults its colour to `{ theme.… }`, so the whole page reskins
from this one place. The scroller fills the viewport and scrolls its taller
content natively; the chrome further down (header, cursor) are its
**siblings**, not its children, so they never move.

segments.html:
App [ appName = "Declare",
    theme = { ({
        // text, brightest to faintest
        text: 0xE7EEF2, lead: 0x9DB0BC, muted: 0x8A9BA6, dim: 0x6A7C89, faint: 0x556673,
        // the blue accent and its interaction shades; links brighten past accent
        accent: 0x4C8DFF, accentHover: 0x63A0FF, accentPress: 0x3D7BE0, linkHover: 0x7FB0FF,
        // the teal second accent; the brand gradient runs accent → accent2
        accent2: 0x37E0C8, accent2Press: 0x9BF2E4,
        brand: gradient("90deg", 0x4C8DFF, 0x37E0C8),
        // grounds, darkest to lightest, and their interaction shades
        ink: 0x0B141B, panel: 0x0C1620, press: 0x0C1922, surfaceDeep: 0x0D1922,
        surface: 0x101E28, hover: 0x162A36, pillHover: 0x1C2D39, pillPress: 0x24384A,
        // rules and strokes
        line: 0x263D4C, panelLine: 0x1E313C, revertLine: 0x26414F, pointRule: 0x345B84,
        grid: 0x172A38 }) },
    fill = { theme.ink },
    fontFamily = [Sans, "system-ui", "sans-serif"], fontSize = 17, fontWeight = normal,
    textColor = { theme.text },

    // in-app location: "home" (the scrolling page), "why" (the mini-article),
    // "language" (the one-file core doc, rendered in-app), or "getstarted" (the
    // quick-start guide, rendered the same way) — the app's slice of the
    // URL (docs/system-design/location.md). `location` is a BUILT-IN App attribute;
    // setting it here declares the INITIAL, which is the default: the host shows a
    // clean URL at "home" and `#why`/`#language` elsewhere, seeds it from a deep
    // link before first paint, and walks it on back/forward — no hash wiring in app
    // code. Every `visible` below derives from it; the header pills write it.

    location = "home",

    // the page's textual material, fetched DIRECTLY as text (format = "text"):
    // the core doc itself (docs/declare.md — up the tree, but still the
    // deployed repo's own material), the getting-started guide, and the FAQ.
    // The authored .md files carry assembler markers as HTML comments, which
    // Markdown drops — so there is no JSON-wrap projection beside this file,
    // and a drifted copy is impossible because there is no copy. stats.json
    // stays JSON (line metrics computed by the prewarm pass, so the figures
    // below can never go stale).

    lang: DataSource [ url = "../../docs/declare.md", format = "text" ],
    stats: DataSource [ url = "stats.json" ],
    guide: DataSource [ url = "getstarted.md", format = "text" ],
    faq: DataSource [ url = "declare-faq.md", format = "text" ],
    onInit() { this.lang.fetch(); this.stats.fetch(); this.guide.fetch(); this.faq.fetch() },

    // the whole-page source editor's state: `editing` toggles the overlay, and
    // `liveCard`/`liveSource` name the demo an inline editor is republishing and
    // its current text — the host recompiles that pair live.

    editing: boolean = false,
    liveCard: string = "",
    liveSource: string = "",

    // the content measure and the centering gutter — the one geometry every
    // aligned edge on the page (column, wordmark, nav) derives from

    measure: number = 1120,
    gutter: number = { Math.max(24, (app.width - app.measure) / 2) },
    heroFont: number = { app.width < 700 ? 44 : 60 },

    scroller: View [ width = { app.width }, height = { app.height }, scrolls = true, visible = { app.location == "home" },

        // full-bleed blueprint behind the column, sized to the page (a percent
        // height stays out of the page's own auto-extent, so no cycle)

        grid: GridBg [ x = 0, y = 0, width = { app.width }, height = { parent.page.height } ],

        // the centered content column: a fixed measure, and a VERTICAL FLOW of
        // sections — each auto-sizes, the page stacks them

        page: View [ width = { Math.min(app.measure, app.width - 48) },
            x = { app.gutter }, y = 96,
            layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = y, spacing = 112 ],

segments.md:
### Hero — the name, the claim, and the two doors in
(GitHub, or edit this page's own source)

segments.html:
hero: View [ width = { parent.width },
                layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = y, spacing = 24 ],
                top: View [ width = { parent.width },
                    layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = y, spacing = 26 ],

                    // the title: product name in the blue→teal gradient, the claim on the
                    // next line. One rendition at every width — no wide/narrow split to keep in sync.

                    titleGroup: View [ width = { parent.width },
                        layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = y, spacing = 12 ],
                        headline: View [ width = { parent.width },
                            layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = y, spacing = 2 ],
                            Heading [ fontSize = { app.heroFont },
                                textFill = { theme.brand }, text = "Declare" ],
                            Heading [ fontSize = { app.heroFont }, width = { parent.width },
                                text = "is the UI language for the AI era." ],
                            ],
                        ],
                    paras: View [ width = { parent.width },
                        layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = y, spacing = 12 ],
                        HTMLText [ width = { Math.min(680, parent.width) }, fontSize = 19, lineHeight = 1.2, bodyColor = { theme.lead }, selectable = true,
                            accents = { ({ g: theme.brand }) },
                            html = "Just as SQL is a domain-specific language for database queries, <span class='g'>Declare</span> is a DSL for user interfaces. Its whole surface fits in your head — and inside an LLM's context window." ],
                        Lead [ width = { Math.min(680, parent.width) },
                            text = "This page is written in it. By an LLM." ],
                        ],
                    ],

                // the two doors in, side by side with a quiet dot between

                learn: View [ width = { parent.width },
                    layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = x, spacing = 16 ],
                    why: Text [ fontFamily = [Sans, "system-ui", "sans-serif"], fontSize = 15, fontWeight = medium, wrap = false,
                        hovered: boolean = false,
                        textColor = { this.hovered ? theme.linkHover : theme.accent }, text = "Why a new language, now? →",
                        onMouseOver() { this.hovered = true },
                        onMouseOut() { this.hovered = false },
                        onClick() { app.location = "why" },
                        ],
                    Dot [ y = 1 ],
                    onefile: Text [ fontFamily = [Sans, "system-ui", "sans-serif"], fontSize = 15, fontWeight = medium, wrap = false,
                        hovered: boolean = false,
                        textColor = { this.hovered ? theme.linkHover : theme.accent }, text = "Declare, in one file, for you and your LLM →",
                        onMouseOver() { this.hovered = true },
                        onMouseOut() { this.hovered = false },
                        onClick() { app.location = "language" },
                        ],
                    ],
                ],

segments.md:
### 01 — Declare Calendar: the reference app leads.
Each numbered section fades in on a Spring the moment it scrolls
into view — a reveal declared as a reactive target, not scheduled.

segments.html:
cal: View [ width = { parent.width }, opacity = 0,
                reveal: Spring [ attribute = opacity, stiffness = 90, damping = 22, epsilon = 0.004,
                    to = { app.scroller.scrollY + app.height > parent.parent.y + parent.y + 90 ? 1 : 0 } ],
                layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = y, spacing = 36 ],

                // the headline stands alone (SectionHead's lead slot would sit ABOVE
                // the actions row — here the CTA comes between headline and copy)

                head: View [ width = { parent.width },
                    layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = y, spacing = 14 ],
                    Marker [ text = "01" ],
                    Heading [ fontSize = 40, width = { Math.min(900, parent.width) },
                        text = "Declare Calendar: Concise. Clear. Continuous." ],
                    ],

                actions: View [ layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = x, spacing = 18 ],
                    Button [ width = 240, label = "Run Declare Calendar →",
                        onClick() { app.navigate("apps/calendar/calendar.declare") },
                        ],
                    srclink: Text [ y = 15, fontFamily = [Sans, "system-ui", "sans-serif"], fontSize = 15, fontWeight = medium, wrap = false,
                        hovered: boolean = false,
                        textColor = { this.hovered ? theme.linkHover : theme.accent }, text = "View source →",
                        onMouseOver() { this.hovered = true },
                        onMouseOut() { this.hovered = false },
                        onClick() { app.navigate("apps/calendar/calendar.declare?viewer") },
                        ],
                    ],

                copy: View [ width = { parent.width },
                    layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = y, spacing = 14 ],
                    Lead [ width = { Math.min(760, parent.width) },
                        text = "It’s the kind of experience you’ll see in the best native apps — a view doesn’t switch so much as become the next one, where motion carries meaning, and everything is fast and interruptible." ],
                    Lead [ width = { Math.min(760, parent.width) },
                        text = "That’s always been specialist craft: bespoke motion code, one interaction at a time, locked to a platform. In Declare, it’s simply what the declarations do, and it works everywhere." ],
                    ],

                // three big gradient figures. The first two are computed, not claimed —
                // stats.json (regenerated every commit) — and the third is the point.

                stat: View [ width = { parent.width },
                    layout: WrappingLayout [ spacing = 48, lineSpacing = 40 ],
                    Metric [ width = { parent.width < 560 ? parent.width : (parent.width - 96) / 3 },
                        value = { "" + (app.stats.read(["calendar", "code"]) ?? "—") },
                        note = "lines of Declare — four views, continuous zoom, drag, and edit" ],
                    Metric [ width = { parent.width < 560 ? parent.width : (parent.width - 96) / 3 },
                        value = { app.stats.read(["calendar", "wireGzip"]) ? Math.round(app.stats.read(["calendar", "wireGzip"]) / 1024) + " KB" : "—" },
                        note = "over the wire, gzipped — the whole app and its runtime" ],
                    Metric [ width = { parent.width < 560 ? parent.width : (parent.width - 96) / 3 },
                        value = "0",
                        note = "lines written by hand — an LLM wrote it; the compiler kept it honest" ],
                    ],
                ],

segments.md: ### 02 — Read it. Generate it. Run it.

segments.html:
gen: View [ width = { parent.width }, opacity = 0,
                reveal: Spring [ attribute = opacity, stiffness = 90, damping = 22, epsilon = 0.004,
                    to = { app.scroller.scrollY + app.height > parent.parent.y + parent.y + 90 ? 1 : 0 } ],
                layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = y, spacing = 40 ],
                SectionHead [ num = "02", head = "Read it. Generate it. Run it.",
                    lead = "The relationships that matter — structure, data, reactivity — are explicit in the language, not hidden in imperative steps or a runtime graph." ],
                cards: View [ width = { parent.width },
                    layout: WrappingLayout [ spacing = 24, lineSpacing = 24 ],
                    Card [ title = "Analyzable", body = "A program's whole structure — components, data, what reacts to what — is right there in the source. The compiler reads it statically; so can an LLM." ],
                    Card [ title = "Predictable", body = "One clear way to write each thing, and the compiler rejects the rest — no dependency arrays, no keys, no stale-value bugs. An LLM's mistake is a compile error that names the fix — not an app that quietly runs wrong." ],
                    Card [ title = "Runnable", body = "The compiler runs in the browser, so code renders the moment it exists — no build, no deploy. Write it and it's live." ],
                    ],
                pull: Heading [ fontSize = 28, width = { parent.width }, textAlign = center,
                    text = "Everything that makes it legible to an LLM makes it legible to you." ],
                ],

segments.md:
### 03 — Declare it. It runs. It stays true.
Sections 03–06 share one shape: a SectionHead, a caption, and a
live SourcePanel + PreviewFrame pair running the demo for real.

segments.html:
s3: View [ width = { parent.width },
                layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = y, spacing = 36 ],
                SectionHead [ num = "03", head = "Declare it. It runs. It stays true.",
                    lead = "A binding isn’t a callback you remember to re-run — it’s a standing relationship the runtime keeps true, re-derived the moment its inputs change." ],
                demo3: View [ width = { parent.width },
                    layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = y, spacing = 14 ],
                    caption: Body [ fontSize = 15, width = { parent.width },
                        text = "Three bindings track one value: the big number, the bar’s width, and its color. Click to bump v — all three update together, and the color crosses cool to warm at the halfway mark." ],
                    cards: View [ width = { parent.width },
                        layout: WrappingLayout [ spacing = 20, lineSpacing = 20 ],
                        SourcePanel  [ editName = "reactivity", height = 226,
                            src = { app.demoSources.reactivity || "" } ],
                        PreviewFrame [ runName  = "reactivity", height = 226 ],
                        ],
                    ],
                ],

segments.md: ### 04 — Declare the shape, not the loop.

segments.html:
s4: View [ width = { parent.width },
                layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = y, spacing = 36 ],
                SectionHead [ num = "04", head = "Declare the shape, not the loop.",
                    lead = "A view can be built from data instead of laid out by hand — the shape written once, the data deciding how many there are and what each one says." ],
                demo4: View [ width = { parent.width },
                    layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = y, spacing = 14 ],
                    caption: Body [ fontSize = 15, width = { parent.width },
                        text = "One Bar, instanced once per row of the data. Edit a number or add a row and the list rebuilds itself — no loop, no keys. And Bar is auto-included: no import, no definition, just use it." ],
                    cards: View [ width = { parent.width },
                        layout: WrappingLayout [ spacing = 20, lineSpacing = 20 ],
                        SourcePanel  [ editName = "components", height = 184,
                            src = { app.demoSources.components || "" } ],
                        PreviewFrame [ runName  = "components", height = 184 ],
                        ],
                    ],
                ],

segments.md: ### 05 — Motion you declare, not schedule.

segments.html:
s5: View [ width = { parent.width },
                layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = y, spacing = 36 ],
                SectionHead [ num = "05", head = "Motion you declare, not schedule.",
                    lead = "You say where a thing belongs; a Spring finds the path there and settles by physics — motion you declare, not a timeline you sequence." ],
                demo5: View [ width = { parent.width },
                    layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = y, spacing = 14 ],
                    caption: Body [ fontSize = 15, width = { parent.width },
                        text = "Click to toggle the ball’s target between two positions. The Spring follows and settles by physics — no timeline, no frames driven by hand." ],
                    cards: View [ width = { parent.width },
                        layout: WrappingLayout [ spacing = 20, lineSpacing = 20 ],
                        SourcePanel  [ editName = "spring", height = 184,
                            src = { app.demoSources.spring || "" } ],
                        PreviewFrame [ runName  = "spring", height = 184 ],
                        ],
                    ],
                ],

segments.md: ### 06 — Declared states, not manual wiring.

segments.html:
s6: View [ width = { parent.width },
                layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = y, spacing = 36 ],
                SectionHead [ num = "06", head = "Declared states, not manual wiring.",
                    lead = "A State is a named bundle of overrides — a whole configuration you can apply as one. The mode is the unit of change, not each property by hand." ],
                demo6: View [ width = { parent.width },
                    layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = y, spacing = 14 ],
                    caption: Body [ fontSize = 15, width = { parent.width },
                        text = "Click to flip one boolean. The card’s height, its color, and an extra line of text swap in together — and revert the instant the mode turns off." ],
                    cards: View [ width = { parent.width },
                        layout: WrappingLayout [ spacing = 20, lineSpacing = 20 ],
                        SourcePanel  [ editName = "states", height = 230,

segments.md: ### The manifesto band — centered, an accent rule above and below

segments.html:
manifesto: View [ width = { parent.width }, opacity = 0,
                reveal: Spring [ attribute = opacity, stiffness = 90, damping = 22, epsilon = 0.004,
                    to = { app.scroller.scrollY + app.height > parent.parent.y + parent.y + 90 ? 1 : 0 } ],
                layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = y, spacing = 30 ],
                ruleTop: View [ x = { (parent.width - 140) / 2 }, width = 140, height = 2, fill = { theme.accent } ],
                Heading [ fontSize = { app.width < 700 ? 26 : 38 },
                    width = { Math.min(900, parent.width) }, x = { (parent.width - Math.min(900, parent.width)) / 2 },
                    textAlign = center,
                    text = "The web’s last generation of frameworks was built on the browser’s document model.\n\nDeclare abstracts the renderer away — you declare what the UI is, and the runtime draws it." ],
                explain: Lead [ width = { Math.min(720, parent.width) }, x = { (parent.width - Math.min(720, parent.width)) / 2 },
                    textAlign = center,
                    text = "The same program paints to the DOM or to a canvas today, WebGL next, with no rewrite. And you never touch the render layer — no DOM to poke, no paint loop to run — so the code stays small: what you write is the interface itself, not the machinery that shows it." ],
                ruleBot: View [ x = { (parent.width - 140) / 2 }, width = 140, height = 2, fill = { theme.accent } ],
                ],

### The closing call to action — the primary blue button that

segments.md:
## The "Why" view

An in-app view (`app.location == "why"`) wearing the same skin as the page —
theme, graph-paper grid, and the header renders here too. The host mirrors
`app.location` to the URL fragment (#why), so it deep-links and honours the
back button — for free, from the one built-in binding. The article itself is
prose in `"""` blocks: HTML collapses the line breaks, so the source wraps
and the page flows.

segments.html:
whyView: View [ visible = { app.location == "why" }, width = { app.width }, height = { app.height }, fill = { theme.ink },
        grid: GridBg [ x = 0, y = 0, width = { app.width }, height = { app.height } ],
        body: View [ y = 56, width = { app.width }, height = { app.height - 56 }, scrolls = true, selectable = true,
            col: View [ width = { Math.min(720, app.width - 48) }, x = { Math.max(24, (app.width - 720) / 2) }, y = 52,
                layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = y, spacing = 22 ],
                Marker  [ text = "WHY DECLARE" ],
                Heading [ fontSize = { app.width < 700 ? 32 : 42 }, width = { parent.width },
                    text = "Why a new language, now?" ],
                Para [ html = """
                    Start with the obvious objection. LLMs write code now, and they're
                    best at the languages they've seen the most — so the winning move is
                    to use whatever has the biggest corpus and let the model figure it
                    out. By that logic the era of new programming languages is over.
                    """ ],
                Para [ html = """
                    That argument gets its facts right and its conclusion backwards.
                    Producing code is now nearly free; trusting it costs what it always
                    did. A model writing React verifies its work by resemblance — this
                    looks like the billion lines it trained on — and resemblance is not
                    correctness. Meanwhile, less and less of the code passes under human
                    eyes, which leaves the language and its compiler as the one reviewer
                    that is always present. Handing the writing to a machine doesn't make
                    the language irrelevant. It makes it load-bearing.
                    """ ],
                Heading [ fontSize = 24, width = { parent.width }, text = "Two kinds of leverage" ],
                Para [ html = """
                    What a model gets from a mainstream stack is corpus leverage: fluency
                    by imitation — broad, shallow, self-contradictory, unverifiable. The
                    other kind is comprehension leverage: a language small enough to hand
                    to a model whole, regular enough that its programs can be analyzed
                    rather than pattern-matched, and backed by a compiler that turns each
                    mistake into a precise correction. Corpus leverage arrives free with
                    popularity. Comprehension leverage has to be designed in — and it is
                    the only kind a new language can compete on.
                    """ ],
                Para [ html = """
                    This is a measured bet, not a hunch. Models can learn a language they
                    never trained on from a spec in context — in recent studies, a small
                    language purpose-designed for LLM use, with zero training exposure,
                    beat the same model writing Python, its best-trained language. And
                    what bounds a model's ability to repair its own mistakes is the
                    quality of the feedback: a compiler that explains beats one that
                    merely rejects, by a double-digit margin.
                    """ ],
                sources: View [ width = { parent.width },
                    layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = x, spacing = 20 ],
                    lbl: Text [ fontSize = 14, wrap = false, textColor = { theme.faint }, text = "The studies:" ],
                    s1: Text [ fontSize = 14, wrap = false,
                        hovered: boolean = false,
                        textColor = { this.hovered ? theme.linkHover : theme.accent }, text = "a zero-corpus DSL vs. Python →",
                        onMouseOver() { this.hovered = true },
                        onMouseOut()  { this.hovered = false },
                        onClick()     { app.navigate("https://arxiv.org/abs/2512.23214") },
                        ],
                    s2: Text [ fontSize = 14, wrap = false,
                        hovered: boolean = false,
                        textColor = { this.hovered ? theme.linkHover : theme.accent }, text = "feedback quality vs. repair →",
                        onMouseOver() { this.hovered = true },
                        onMouseOut()  { this.hovered = false },
                        onClick()     { app.navigate("https://arxiv.org/abs/2504.06939") },
                        ],
                    ],
                Heading [ fontSize = 24, width = { parent.width }, text = "Designed in, not bolted on" ],
                Para [ html = """
                    Declare's declarative layer is small — the whole language fits in a
                    model's context window with room left for your app — and everything
                    inside a { } is ordinary TypeScript, so the hard part of generation
                    rides the largest corpus there is. Where Declare looks familiar, it
                    behaves the way a model — or you — will assume: assignment is
                    assignment, and there is no bypass to forget. Where it's genuinely
                    new, it looks new — brackets for structure, :path for data — a
                    visible cue to consult the spec instead of autocompleting from memory.
                    """ ],
                Para [ html = """
                    And because a UI's real structure — components, state, data, what
                    reacts to what — is expressed in the language, the compiler can see
                    all of it: it extracts every binding's dependencies statically,
                    checks the shape of the tree, and types the seams. What a model
                    reasons about is exactly what the compiler verifies, and its errors
                    are written for that loop — each one states the rule and names the
                    rewrite that fixes it, because the primary reader of a compiler
                    message, now, is a model deciding what to do next.
                    """ ],
                Heading [ fontSize = 24, width = { parent.width }, text = "What it's actually for" ],
                Para [ html = """
                    All of that is the floor, not the point. The point is what a language
                    makes sayable. The most prized layer of modern UI — the continuity
                    you feel in the best native software, where a view doesn't switch so
                    much as become the next one, where motion carries meaning and
                    everything stays interruptible — has always been bespoke: specialist
                    craft, one interaction at a time, locked to a platform. It is also
                    the least machine-writable code there is; the corpus is thin, custom,
                    and wrong in ways nothing catches.
                    """ ],
                Para [ html = """
                    Declare makes continuity the grain, not the garnish. Motion is a
                    Spring on an attribute; layout is a reactive slot; a mode is a
                    reversible state — so the continuous version of an interface is often
                    less code than the discrete one. The reference app is a calendar
                    whose four views are one surface seen through a moving, zooming
                    rectangle — normally a bespoke project of its own, here a few hundred
                    lines of declarations, readable end to end, written with an LLM. The
                    whole category moves into the declarative, analyzable layer: the
                    hardest part of ambitious UX becomes the part a model is best at.
                    """ ],
                Para [ html = """
                    And that is the forward-looking wager. We believe there is another
                    generation of experiences past the carefully crafted ones we point to
                    today — built on a foundation with continuity at its core, enabled at
                    the language level, deployed as universally as a URL, and iterated by
                    imaginative designer-developers working with a model at their side.
                    Declare is built to find that out.
                    """ ],
                Para [ html = """
                    None of this trades away the human. What makes code legible to a
                    model is what makes it fast for you to read — and you are still the
                    reader who decides. You don't teach an LLM Declare with a million
                    examples. You hand it the language and let the compiler keep it
                    honest. The corpus will come; the language doesn't need it first.
                    """ ],
                gap: View [ width = 8, height = 64 ],
                Footer [ width = { parent.width } ],
                tail: View [ width = 8, height = 40 ],
                ],
            ],
        ],

segments.md:
## The "Language" view

The one-file core doc (docs/declare.md), rendered in-app by the native
Markdown component — same skin and same location machinery as the Why
article, so it deep-links (#language) and is crawled into the static
document like any other reachable location. The text arrives as the
app's own material — docs/declare.md fetched directly, format = "text":
loading and failure are lifecycle states the view derives from, never
a blank.

segments.html:
languageView: View [ visible = { app.location == "language" }, width = { app.width }, height = { app.height }, fill = { theme.ink },
        grid: GridBg [ x = 0, y = 0, width = { app.width }, height = { app.height } ],
        body: View [ y = 56, width = { app.width }, height = { app.height - 56 }, scrolls = true, selectable = true,
            col: View [ width = { Math.min(720, app.width - 48) }, x = { Math.max(24, (app.width - 720) / 2) }, y = 52,
                layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = y, spacing = 22 ],
                Marker [ text = "THE LANGUAGE" ],
                loading: Text [ visible = { app.lang.loading }, fontSize = 15, textColor = { theme.faint }, text = "Loading the language…" ],
                failed:  Text [ visible = { app.lang.failed }, fontSize = 15, textColor = { theme.faint }, wrap = true, width = { parent.width },
                    text = { "The language file did not load — " + (app.lang.error || "") } ],
                doc: Markdown [ visible = { app.lang.loaded }, width = { parent.width }, lineHeight = 1.35, bodyColor = { theme.lead }, dark = true,
                    text = { app.lang.value || "" } ],
                gap: View [ width = 8, height = 64 ],
                Footer [ width = { parent.width } ],
                tail: View [ width = 8, height = 40 ],
                ],
            ],
        ],

segments.md:
## The "Get Started" view

The getting-started guide (app.location == "getstarted"), rendered in-app
with the same skin and Markdown pipeline as the Language view: the copy is
getstarted.md itself (an authored page, its command blocks generated), so
it deep-links (#getstarted), honours the back button, and is crawled into
the static document like any other reachable location. Loading and failure
are lifecycle states the view derives from, never a blank.

segments.html:
getStartedView: View [ visible = { app.location == "getstarted" }, width = { app.width }, height = { app.height }, fill = { theme.ink },
        grid: GridBg [ x = 0, y = 0, width = { app.width }, height = { app.height } ],
        body: View [ y = 56, width = { app.width }, height = { app.height - 56 }, scrolls = true, selectable = true,
            col: View [ width = { Math.min(720, app.width - 48) }, x = { Math.max(24, (app.width - 720) / 2) }, y = 52,
                layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = y, spacing = 22 ],
                Marker [ text = "GETTING STARTED" ],
                loading: Text [ visible = { app.guide.loading }, fontSize = 15, textColor = { theme.faint }, text = "Loading the guide…" ],
                failed:  Text [ visible = { app.guide.failed }, fontSize = 15, textColor = { theme.faint }, wrap = true, width = { parent.width },
                    text = { "The guide did not load — " + (app.guide.error || "") } ],
                doc: Markdown [ visible = { app.guide.loaded }, width = { parent.width }, lineHeight = 1.35, bodyColor = { theme.lead }, dark = true,
                    text = { app.guide.value || "" } ],

segments.md:
## Fixed chrome

Siblings of the scroller, so they never scroll: the top bar (hidden until
you scroll past the hero, then two Springs bring it in — the declarative
form of a timed entrance), the whole-page editor overlay, and the pointer
dot that springs after the cursor.

segments.html:
// the bar is ALWAYS present, stuck to the top — a sibling of the
    // scroller, so it never scrolls (the springs that used to reveal it on
    // scroll retired 2026-07-17: a first-time visitor sees the whole map)

    header: View [ width = { app.width }, height = 56, y = 0, opacity = 1, fill = { theme.surface },
        hairline: View [ y = 55, width = { app.width }, height = 1, fill = { theme.line } ],

        // wordmark — the product name in the brand gradient, in the hero's typeface

        brand: View [ x = { app.gutter }, y = 18, height = 22,
            layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = x, spacing = 0 ],
            onClick() { app.location = "home" },
            Heading [ fontSize = 15, textFill = { theme.brand }, text = "Declare" ],
            ],

        // primary nav — auto-width, right-aligned to the same gutter the content
        // ends at (x = right edge − own width); the padded pills carry the spacing

        nav: View [ y = 11, x = { app.width - app.gutter - this.width },
            layout: SimpleLayout [ axis = x, spacing = 2 ],
            NavLink [ label = "Getting Started", to = "getstarted" ],
            NavLink [ label = "Why Declare", to = "why" ],

            // FAQ sits right after the pitch — the question-form follow-on a
            // new visitor reaches for before the deep artifacts

            NavLink [ label = "FAQ", to = "faq" ],

            // redundant at phone width (section 02 links it) — drop it before the nav rides the wordmark

            NavLink [ label = "Calendar", url = "apps/calendar/calendar.declare", visible = { app.width >= 620 } ],

            // desktop drops first as the window narrows — the widest link, kept
            // only while the nav clears the wordmark

            NavLink [ label = "Desktop", url = "apps/desktop/desktop.declare", visible = { app.width >= 760 } ],
            NavLink [ label = "Docs", url = "apps/docs/docs.declare" ],
            ],
        ],

    // the whole-page source editor, shown when app.editing (flipped by
    // "View & Edit Source" in the footer and "Edit this page" in the hero)

    editor: FullPageEditor [ open = { app.editing },
        pageSource = { app.demoSources.__page__ || "" } ],

    // pointer dot — a 10px accent-2 dot in VIEWPORT space that SPRINGS after the
    // pointer (loose + low-mass = the slower, longer trail). Two position
    // Springs; the native cursor stays visible and leads, the dot follows.

    pointerDot: View [ width = 10, height = 10, cornerRadius = 5, fill = { theme.accent2 }, opacity = 0.9,
        shadow = { shadow(0, 0, 12, theme.accent2) },

        // Only for a HOVERING pointer (mouse / trackpad) — `app.hovering` is false
        // for touch, so the dot never appears on a phone, yet an iPad trackpad
        // (which reports a "mouse" pointer) still gets it. Also yields to the
        // native I-beam when the pointer is over an editable field.

        visible = { app.hovering && !app.pointerOverText },
        followX: Spring [ attribute = x, to = { app.pointerX - 5 },
            stiffness = 120, damping = 14, mass = 0.6, epsilon = 0.05 ],
        followY: Spring [ attribute = y, to = { app.pointerY - 5 },
            stiffness = 120, damping = 14, mass = 0.6, epsilon = 0.05 ],
        ],
    ]
